title: Week 1 Internship Log (February 4–6, 2025)
summary: Completed introductory Sui and Move programming modules while developing UI/UX website templates for Makerspace Innovhub.
author: Michelle T. Fernandez
content:
## Objectives

This week focused on:

- Acquiring foundational knowledge of Sui and Move programming languages
- Applying theoretical concepts in website design template development
- Enhancing competencies in UI/UX design for web-based platforms

## What I Learned

This week I studied:

- Introduction to Sui blockchain technology
- Fundamentals of Move programming language
- UI/UX design principles
- Website layout and template research
- Responsive design planning

## Accomplishments

During the week, I successfully:

- Completed Module 1: Introduction to Sui
- Progressed in Module 2: Introduction to Move with practical exercises
- Conducted research on effective website design templates
- Designed the Contact Us page template for the Makerspace Innovhub website
- Continued developing the Business page template

## Reflection

The objectives for the week were achieved through structured learning and practical application. Completing the introductory Sui module and engaging in Move programming exercises provided a strong theoretical foundation. Applying these concepts through website design tasks strengthened both technical proficiency and creative problem-solving skills.

## Realizations

This week taught me the importance of continuous learning and applying newly acquired knowledge in practical scenarios.

I developed skills in:

- UI/UX Design
- Research and planning
- Adaptability
- Problem-solving
- Blockchain fundamentals

## Technologies Used

- Sui, Move, Figma, Next.js, Tailwind CSS

title: Week 2 Internship Log (February 16–20, 2026)
summary: Developed and refined a vehicle comparison webpage design template while applying UI/UX principles and iterative design improvements.
author: Michelle T. Fernandez
content:
## Objectives

This week focused on:

- Developing a comprehensive design template for a vehicle comparison webpage
- Applying UI/UX design principles in creating structured and user-friendly layouts
- Refining and improving design outputs through revisions and feedback implementation

## What I Learned

This week I studied and practiced:

- Advanced UI/UX layout structuring
- Comparison page design strategies
- Content hierarchy and readability
- Iterative design improvement workflows
- Feedback implementation and revision techniques

## Accomplishments

During the week, I successfully:

- Initiated the creation of a design template for the **2026 Hyundai Sonata vs. 2026 Nissan Altima** comparison webpage
- Developed the initial layout and page structure
- Enhanced the overall design and content organization
- Implemented revisions to improve visual presentation and usability
- Successfully completed all required revisions for the comparison page design

## Reflection

The objectives for the week were accomplished through a systematic workflow involving design development and continuous refinement. The process started with creating an initial design structure and progressed through multiple revisions based on identified improvements and feedback.

## Realizations

This week’s activities emphasized the importance of iterative design and continuous improvement in producing high-quality digital outputs.

Through these tasks, I developed stronger competencies in UI/UX Design, Critical evaluation, and User-centered design thinking.

## Technologies Used

- Figma, UI/UX Design Principles, Responsive Layout Design, Website Wireframing

title: Week 3 Internship Log (February 23–27, 2026)
summary: Enhanced automotive comparison webpage templates, improved responsive layouts, explored SyncSnap project technologies, and gained hands-on production experience.
author: Michelle T. Fernandez
content:
## Objectives

This week focused on:

- Designing and enhancing comparison webpage templates for automotive content
- Improving responsiveness and layout structure of web pages
- Exploring tools, technologies, and system design concepts for the SyncSnap project
- Gaining hands-on experience in production tasks such as heat press transfer printing

## What I Learned

This week I studied and practiced:

- Responsive web design techniques
- UI/UX improvements for comparison pages
- System workflow planning and brainstorming
- Technology stack evaluation for web applications
- Production workflows involving heat press transfer printing

## Accomplishments

During the week, I successfully:

- Created a design template for the **2026 Chevrolet Silverado 1500 vs. 2026 Toyota Tundra** comparison page
- Improved responsiveness and overall page layout structure
- Revised and enhanced the **2026 Hyundai Sonata vs. 2026 Nissan Altima** comparison page
- Assisted in producing customized t-shirts and mugs using heat press transfer printing
- Researched and evaluated tools and technologies for the SyncSnap project

## Reflection

The objectives for the week were achieved through a combination of design development, iterative improvements, and exploratory research. Creating and refining webpage templates strengthened my ability to apply UI/UX principles, especially in ensuring responsiveness, usability, and visual consistency.

## Realizations

This week’s tasks highlighted the importance of adaptability, continuous learning, and attention to detail in producing high-quality outputs.

I gained a deeper appreciation for both the digital and physical aspects of project development.

## Technologies Used

- Figma, Responsive Web Design, UI/UX Principles, System Planning, Technology Stack Research

title: Week 4 Internship Log (March 2–6, 2026)
summary: Enhanced SyncSnap UI/UX components, improved dashboard and workspace modules, and implemented database structures and interactive system features.
author: Michelle T. Fernandez
content:
## Objectives

This week focused on:

- Designing and enhancing user interface components for the SyncSnap application
- Improving user experience through layout refinement and usability enhancements
- Developing and organizing core system functionalities including database structures
- Implementing additional features that improve system efficiency and interactivity

## What I Learned

This week I studied and practiced:

- Database structure organization
- Advanced dashboard UI refinement
- Workspace management interfaces
- Password validation and strength indicators
- UI consistency and interaction patterns

## Accomplishments

During the week, I successfully:

- Created database tables for core system functionalities
- Improved the UI/UX of landing and authentication pages
- Enhanced workspace creation and joining modals
- Integrated a password strength indicator in the registration page
- Improved the Settings module including Profile, Password, and Appearance sections
- Redesigned the Standup Form modal in Dashboard and Workspace modules
- Enhanced Dashboard UI components such as Blockers, Streak, Bonuses, and Workspaces
- Implemented sorting, filtering, and grid/list layout options for workspace cards

## Reflection

This week’s activities strengthened my understanding of frontend and backend integration in building user-centered systems. Developing database structures and refining interface components improved my ability to create efficient and intuitive application workflows while maintaining usability and consistency.

## Technologies Used

- Next.js, Tailwind CSS, UI/UX Design, Database Structuring, Dashboard Systems

author: Michelle T. Fernandez
content:

- Enhancing and refining user interface components within the SyncSnap application
- Improving system usability through layout adjustments and feature optimization

This week I explored:

- UI consistency techniques

The continuous refinement of system components improved my attention to detail, problem-solving abilities, and understanding of user-centered design. I learned the importance of maintaining organized and intuitive interfaces that improve usability and workflow efficiency.

title: Week 8 Internship Log (April 6–10, 2026)
summary: Initiated the FlowState application with authentication, MongoDB integration, dashboard systems, and collaborative team management features.
author: Michelle T. Fernandez
content:
## Objectives

This week focused on:

- Initiating development of the FlowState web application
- Establishing database connectivity and user data management
- Implementing authentication and team management features
- Enhancing navigation and usability

## What I Learned

This week I studied and practiced:

- Full-stack application setup
- MongoDB Atlas integration
- Authentication workflows
- Team collaboration systems
- Dashboard architecture

## Accomplishments

During the week, I successfully:

- Set up a Next.js project with App Router and Tailwind CSS
- Connected the application to MongoDB Atlas
- Developed Login and Signup authentication pages
- Implemented user data storage functionality
- Built the initial dashboard layout
- Added team creation and joining functionality using invite codes
- Added team switching through dropdown navigation
- Implemented collapsible sidebar navigation systems

## Reflection

This week strengthened my understanding of integrating frontend and backend technologies into a scalable system. Implementing authentication and team-based features improved my knowledge of application structure, user management, and intuitive navigation design.

## Technologies Used

- Next.js, MongoDB Atlas, Tailwind CSS, Authentication Systems, Dashboard Development

title: Week 9 Internship Log (April 13–17, 2026)
summary: Developed task and blocker management systems with CRUD operations, permissions, and improved workflow management features.
author: Michelle T. Fernandez
content:
## Objectives

This week focused on:

- Developing task and blocker management functionalities
- Improving system accuracy through bug fixes and data handling
- Refining user interface clarity and usability
- Implementing role-based controls and permissions

## What I Learned

This week I practiced:

- CRUD operation implementation
- Role-based access control
- Workflow management systems
- Task and blocker tracking
- API integration and state management

## Accomplishments

During the week, I successfully:

- Developed Daily Stand-up functionality with data storage
- Implemented task fetching for My Tasks and All Tasks views
- Added CRUD functionality for tasks and blockers
- Implemented role-based edit and delete restrictions
- Added task and blocker badges with comment functionality
- Improved blocker status handling and permissions
- Refined My Tasks and dashboard UI modules

## Reflection

This week emphasized the importance of system accuracy, secure permissions, and intuitive workflow management. I strengthened my ability to implement scalable CRUD systems while ensuring usability, security, and clear interface organization.

## Technologies Used

- CRUD Operations, REST APIs, Role-Based Permissions, Task Management Systems, UI/UX Design

title: Week 10 Internship Log (April 20–24, 2026)
summary: Implemented messaging systems, file uploads, pagination, and performance optimizations within the FlowState application.
author: Michelle T. Fernandez
content:
## Objectives

This week focused on:

- Enhancing communication and real-time interaction features
- Improving performance through optimization techniques
- Implementing messaging, file handling, and pagination systems
- Refining user interfaces for consistency and usability

## What I Learned

This week I explored:

- Real-time messaging systems
- Infinite scroll pagination
- File upload handling
- Backend optimization techniques
- Advanced dashboard refinement

## Accomplishments

During the week, I successfully:

- Implemented automatic SOD and EOD reporting in Team Feed chatboxes
- Added reply functionality and @mentions in chat systems
- Integrated file and image uploads with previews and captions
- Implemented infinite scroll pagination using React Virtuoso
- Optimized backend message APIs with efficient query handling
- Added search and filtering functionality across modules
- Designed the AI Insight Panel and Team Activity page
- Improved Dashboard and Settings module UI consistency

## Reflection

This week highlighted the importance of balancing system performance, collaboration features, and user experience in application development. I strengthened my skills in scalable feature implementation, optimization techniques, and responsive communication systems.

## Technologies Used

- React Virtuoso, Real-Time Messaging Systems, Pagination Systems, Backend Optimization, File Upload Systems, Dashboard Development

title: Week 11 Internship Log (April 27 – May 2, 2026)
summary: Enhanced FlowState with real-time messaging, secure authentication, workstream management, and optimized dashboard performance.
author: Michelle T. Fernandez
content:
## Objectives

This week focused on:

- Enhancing FlowState through real-time communication and collaboration features
- Improving user profile management and settings functionalities
- Optimizing dashboard performance, API efficiency, and data handling
- Refining user interface layouts for better usability, consistency, and responsiveness
- Implementing secure authentication and workstream management features

## What I Learned

This week I explored and practiced:

- WebSocket communication using Socket.IO
- Real-time chat systems and synchronization
- Secure authentication with bcrypt hashing
- Workstream management and role-based workflows
- Dashboard optimization and API efficiency improvements
- Profile management and MongoDB file handling

## Accomplishments

During the week, I successfully:

- Enhanced the overall Dashboard UI for improved visual consistency
- Redesigned the Team Activity page timeline with clearer activity hierarchy
- Added overview cards for quick insights and blocker visibility
- Improved Team Feed UI and resolved panel movement issues
- Applied edit restrictions to SOD/EOD reports
- Extended the signup flow with employment type, job roles, skills, resume upload, and profile photo storage in MongoDB
- Updated user API routes to correctly return and persist profile data
- Redesigned the Settings page with edit/view toggles and modern layouts
- Implemented profile photo upload and update functionality
- Integrated backend profile data into the sidebar for real-time updates
- Fixed login issues using bcrypt password hashing and secure verification
- Added resume upload, preview, and deletion functionalities
- Resolved dashboard data overload caused by duplicate API requests and retry loops
- Implemented private workstream functionality with admin-controlled member assignment
- Added full CRUD operations for workstreams including member management and deletion
- Set up a custom Next.js server with Socket.IO for WebSocket communication
- Enabled real-time messaging for chats and workstreams
- Replaced polling with WebSockets to improve responsiveness and efficiency
- Fixed message duplication issues for accurate real-time synchronization

## Reflection

The objectives for this week were achieved through the integration of communication systems, authentication security, and backend optimization within the FlowState application. Implementing WebSocket-based messaging improved real-time collaboration while secure authentication strengthened system reliability and user protection.

title: Week 12 Internship Log (May 4–9, 2026)
summary: Enhanced workstream management, role-based permissions, Socket.IO synchronization, and Daily Standup workflows within the FlowState application.
author: Michelle T. Fernandez
content:
## Objectives

This week focused on:

- Enhancing workstream management and role-based permissions
- Improving real-time synchronization and collaboration using Socket.IO
- Optimizing task management and blocker handling
- Refining user interface layouts for improved readability and navigation
- Enhancing Daily Standup and reporting workflows

## What I Learned

This week I explored and practiced:

- Advanced Socket.IO synchronization
- Workstream visibility and permission handling
- Real-time dashboard updates
- Team collaboration workflows
- Task and blocker automation systems
- Role management and API permission controls

## Accomplishments

During the week, I successfully:

- Enhanced Daily Standup workflows with Workstream selection support
- Fixed missing task, blocker, and focus area information in Team Feed messages
- Improved the Daily Summary Panel with filtering and API fixes
- Added workstream columns and filters in My Tasks
- Refined Dashboard statistics visibility based on workstreams
- Enhanced Team Activity with filtering and improved status indicators
- Updated backend APIs to support workstream data and visibility logic
- Implemented real-time Socket.IO updates across multiple modules
- Redesigned the AI Insights interface with Overview, AI Digest, Risk Monitor, Sprint Planner, Bug & Security, and Standup tabs
- Redesigned Edit Workstream Members UI with improved search functionality
- Added admin role assignment and member management features
- Implemented automatic carryover for unfinished blockers
- Added reactions for message bubbles and SOD/EOD reports
- Improved reactor hover tooltips with avatar and name displays
- Optimized real-time synchronization for workstream activities
- Refined My Tasks layouts with cleaner organization and spacing

## Reflection

The objectives for this week were achieved through continuous improvements in workstream management, role-based permissions, and real-time synchronization within the FlowState application.

title: Week 13 Internship Log (May 11–14, 2026)
summary: Integrated AI-powered analytics, deadline monitoring, and advanced reporting systems into the FlowState application.
author: Michelle T. Fernandez
content:
## Objectives

This week focused on:

- Enhancing AI Insights and analytics capabilities
- Improving task and blocker management with intelligent suggestions and deadline tracking
- Refining reporting and visualization features for better monitoring
- Optimizing dashboard analytics and responsive UI components
- Strengthening PDF export and reporting functionalities

## What I Learned

This week I explored and practiced:

- AI-powered analytics integration
- Productivity and burnout monitoring systems
- Advanced data visualization techniques
- Deadline risk prediction systems
- PDF report generation workflows
- Team productivity analytics

## Accomplishments

During the week, I successfully:

- Fixed Team Activity API logic with actor tracking and dedicated timestamps
- Improved Team Panel UI and Tasks/Blockers activity designs
- Resolved My Tasks auto carry-over issues
- Enhanced Landing Page Hero cursor effects and FAQ section
- Integrated Gemini AI into the AI Insights page for daily, weekly, and monthly summaries
- Added workstream selectors for report filtering
- Built a Members tab displaying task progress and blockers with progress bars
- Added downloadable PDF report support using react-to-print
- Added deadline fields to Add Task and Add Blocker modals
- Implemented AI-generated assignee suggestions
- Designed a Risk & Productivity Monitor with Burnout Detection and Deadline Risk Prediction
- Added risk classification systems for team members
- Fixed graph rendering issues in downloadable PDF exports
- Enhanced analytics with stacked bar charts and trend area charts
- Added workstream health lists and performance gauge rings
- Improved Dashboard AI Insight panels with updated graphs and analytics

## Reflection

The objectives for this week were achieved through the integration of AI-powered insights, analytical dashboards, and intelligent productivity monitoring systems within the FlowState application.

title: Week 14 Internship Log (May 18–19, 2026)
summary: Focused on improving FlowState UI components, refining Sprint Planner layouts, completing system documentation, and finalizing personal portfolio enhancements.
author: Michelle T. Fernandez
content:
## Objectives

This week focused on:

- Refining the user interface and layout consistency across FlowState modules
- Improving Sprint Planner organization and usability
- Fixing data-fetching issues in team member monitoring
- Completing the official system documentation and presentation materials
- Continuing the development and enhancement of the personal portfolio website

## What I Learned

This week I explored and practiced:

- Advanced responsive UI structuring and alignment techniques
- Better table layout organization and dashboard composition
- Side panel integration for AI-generated recommendations
- Documentation preparation for system turnover and future developers
- Portfolio UI/UX improvements and interactive design concepts

## Accomplishments

During the week, I successfully:

- Improved UI alignment in the AI Insights page, including updates to the Monitor tab and Sprint Planner section
- Fixed member profile fetching issues in the Members tab
- Enhanced Sprint Planner tables for Blockers and Regular Tasks with cleaner styling and more consistent layouts
- Moved AI Sprint Recommendations into a dedicated side panel for improved organization and usability
- Completed the official FlowState system documentation
- Created and finalized the FlowState PowerPoint presentation for project turnover
- Improved responsive layouts and dashboard spacing across multiple sections
- Finalized several updates and enhancements for my personal portfolio website
- Continued improving interactive UI elements and overall visual consistency

## Reflection

The objectives for this week were successfully achieved through continuous refinement of the FlowState system interface, improved Sprint Planner organization, and completion of the project documentation requirements. The experience strengthened my understanding of responsive UI design, system turnover preparation, and frontend optimization. Additionally, working on my personal portfolio allowed me to further enhance my skills in modern web design, layout composition, and interactive user experiences.
